issues.description: The new functions `focusOnBounds` and `focusOnEntities` are defined inside `createSelection` but are not added to either the returned object (line 262: `return { setHovered, setSelected, refreshSelected, fitToView, flyToEntity, invalidateRect, dispose }`) or to the `SelectionContext` interface (lines 29-37). As a result they are unreachable from any caller — the measurement tool the doc-comment references cannot call them. This makes the change effectively dead code and the stated purpose unachievable.
issues.suggested_fix: Add `focusOnBounds` and `focusOnEntities` to the `SelectionContext` interface and to the `return { ... }` object at the bottom of `createSelection`. Also expose them via `gui/src/viewport/index.ts` if the measurement tool imports from there.

issues.description: The diff hunk header reads `export function createSelectionManager(` but the actual function in the file is `createSelection` (line 46). Either the diff was produced against a stale copy or a parallel rename happened. Applying this patch against the real source is not affected (hunk headers are informational), but it suggests the author may be editing a different version of the file than the one on disk — worth verifying before merge.
issues.suggested_fix: Regenerate the patch against the current `createSelection` source, or confirm the rename is intended and update all references consistently.

issues.description: `focusOnBounds` duplicates the camera-positioning math already present in both `fitToView` (lines 189-218) and `flyToEntity` (lines 220-246). All three now carry the same `center/size/maxDim/fovRad/distance/viewDir` block. `fitToView` and `flyToEntity` should delegate to `focusOnBounds` (the latter passing padding=1.0 to preserve current behavior) to eliminate the triple duplication.
issues.suggested_fix: Refactor `fitToView` to compute a `Box3` over all meshes and call `focusOnBounds(box, 1.0)`. Refactor `flyToEntity` to build its Box3 and call `focusOnBounds(box, 1.0)`. This also makes the padding policy a single knob.

issues.description: `focusOnBounds` defaults `padding` to 1.2, while the existing `fitToView`/`flyToEntity` effectively use 1.0. When the measurement tool calls `focusOnEntities`, framing will visibly differ from the existing fit/fly behavior. Decide on one project-wide padding convention or make padding explicit at every call site so the difference is intentional.
issues.suggested_fix: Either drop the 1.2 default (require explicit padding) or update `fitToView`/`flyToEntity` to use the same default after refactoring — whichever matches product intent.

issues.description: `focusOnEntities` calls `getMeshes()` once per entity path inside the loop. `getMeshes` is a closure getter that likely reads from the mesh manager — cheap today, but across large selections this allocates iterators/maps repeatedly. Hoist the map reference outside the loop to match the pattern used in `setHovered` (line 132) and `raycast` (line 75).
issues.suggested_fix: Change to `const meshes = getMeshes(); for (const path of entityPaths) { const mesh = meshes.get(path); ... }`.

issues.description: The companion test file covers `flyToEntity` and friends but the diff adds no tests for `focusOnBounds` or `focusOnEntities`. Untested behavior includes: empty entity list, all paths missing from mesh map, mixed hit/miss paths, bounds.isEmpty early return, padding math, and controls.target sync. Given the tool-targeted use case (measurement tool focusing on picked edges), these edge cases are likely.
issues.suggested_fix: Add unit tests modeled after the existing `createSelection returns object with flyToEntity method` block — verify camera position/target are updated for a known Box3, and that empty/missing-path inputs are no-ops.

issues.description: The JSDoc says `Used by the measurement tool to focus on measured geometry`, but as noted above nothing currently imports or calls `focusOnBounds`. Either the caller is missing from this diff, or the comment is aspirational. Either way a reviewer reading the file today will be misled.
issues.suggested_fix: Land the measurement-tool wiring in the same PR, or reword the doc to `Intended for use by the measurement tool (wired up in task #N).`

issues.description: `focusOnBounds` does not guard against a degenerate (zero-size) bounding box, e.g. a single measured point or coincident vertices. `maxDim` becomes 0, `distance` becomes 0, and the camera snaps to the center with no offset, leaving the user with nothing on-screen. `fitToView`/`flyToEntity` inherit the same bug but the measurement tool is much more likely to pass zero-extent bounds (points, degenerate segments).
issues.suggested_fix: Clamp `maxDim` to a minimum (e.g. `Math.max(maxDim, EPSILON)` or use the scene scale / a configured minimum focus distance) before computing distance.

comprehensive_reviewer.summary: The diff adds `focusOnBounds` and `focusOnEntities` helpers to `createSelection`, but neither is added to the returned `SelectionContext` or the interface — so they are unreachable dead code and the measurement tool cannot actually call them (blocking). The hunk header also names a non-existent `createSelectionManager`, suggesting a stale base. Beyond wiring, the new functions duplicate the camera-positioning math already present in `fitToView` and `flyToEntity` (should be refactored to delegate), introduce an inconsistent 1.2 padding default, call `getMeshes()` inside a loop, have no tests, and share a degenerate-bounds bug that will bite the measurement tool's point/edge use case.
